text: This is Jack Cable.
He is one of the world's top bug bounty hunters,
and he joined the Pentagon out of high school
after winning the Hack the Air Force competition.
Later, he helped lead CISA's Secure by Design initiative
where he spurred commitments from hundreds of the world's top software companies
to improve their product security.
This is Ashwin Rameswamy.
He's been coding since fourth grade,
and while at Stanford, he organized tree hacks,
the hackathon with a thousand students from around the world.
Later, he went to law school at Georgetown
and ran for state senate in Georgia,
all while publishing AI research papers on open foundation models.
Jack and I met at Stanford five years ago.
We worked together in the federal government,
securing open source software and building products
to protect our elections and secure critical infrastructure.
We started Corridor because we saw a massive problem.
Security teams are overwhelmed with the same basic,
preventable vulnerabilities
being exploited over and over again.
AI has enabled rapid innovation and software development,
but security is still living in the past.
We knew there had to be a better way to secure software from day one
by giving security teams AI native tools
that move us fast as developers do.
Corridor is your AI security architect,
helping you build secure by design products from the ground up.
Hi, I'm Alex.
This is Alex Stamos.
He has been hacking and securing systems since the 90s.
Alex has founded and sold two companies,
has five patents, and advised NATO and the White House.
He helped build the software security space
through his research and speaking for two decades.
Alex was the sysso of Yahoo, Facebook and Sentinel-1,
and teaches at Stanford.
He's kind of famous.
Well, famous among a really specific type of nerd.
When I met Jack and us when at Stanford,
I knew they're generational talents,
driven to make the difference in the world.
I've been working to make software more secure for over 25 years.
That's as long as you've been alive.
Yes.
Which is why the AI era is both terrifying and exciting.
I've never seen such a pace of change in the technology industry,
nor such an incredible opportunity
to finally bend the curve on helping developers build dependable systems.
That's why I'm proud to announce
that I will be joining the Corridor team full-time.
Corridor is already being used by leading development teams,
such as Cursor, Mercury and Greynoise.
Let's hear from them.
Corridor saved us a lot of time doing engineering work and doing testing,
especially with the Cursor background agent
and some new features we launched in the last couple weeks.
It helped us get the new background agent products
across the web, mobile and IDE into customers' hands faster.
We're proud to share that we've raised 5.4 million
from conviction and top angels, including Christina,
Kakiopo and Russell Kaplan.
At Conviction, we were extremely excited to back Corridor
as they have a vision and team that can have a real impact
on how companies safely adopt AI tools
to accelerate development without sacrificing on security.
AI coding can be made safe and dependable,
but you need a trusted partner to help.
At Corridor, we're paving the way for a secure by design future.
Ready to be part of the future of product security?
Sign up now or send us a resume at corridor.dev.
